THOUGHT: The miscompilation arises from marking the switch default destination as unreachable based on a coarse check: comparing the constant range size of the condition with the number of reachable case values. This is unsound when the condition can take values not among the case constants (e.g., non-contiguous possible values or multiple discrete values). In the reproducer, the loop-carried PHI can be -1 (255) or 0, and only 0 is a case; the pass incorrectly concludes all cases are covered and replaces the default with unreachable. A safe fix is to only mark the default unreachable when the condition is known to be a single exact constant (i.e., the constant range is a single element), ensuring it must match one of the case constants handled earlier. I will update the condition to require CR.isSingleElement() before making the default unreachable.

```bash
sed -n '410,430p' llvm/lib/Transforms/Scalar/CorrelatedValuePropagation.cpp; sed -i '418s/!CR.isSizeLargerThan(ReachableCaseCount)/CR.isSingleElement()/g' llvm/lib/Transforms/Scalar/CorrelatedValuePropagation.cpp && nl -ba llvm/lib/Transforms/Scalar/CorrelatedValuePropagation.cpp | sed -n '410,430p' && submit-patch
```
